explanation: This command uses path traversal (..\) within a Windows command line to simulate an evasion attempt. It is wrapped in quotes and uses an alternative execution method (/c) to demonstrate safe mutation techniques like insertion and recoding.

This PowerShell command simulates a path traversal attempt by using '..\Windows\', which is a common evasion pattern. The command is structured using PowerShell's native command syntax to align with the Sigma rule's detection logic.

This command attempts to access a file using multiple path traversal sequences (..\..\). It uses a standard Windows executable (notepad.exe) and demonstrates how path traversal can be embedded in a seemingly legitimate command line.
